news.summary: In Oslo, Norway as of May 8, 2025, Statkraft reported robust performance in Q1 2025. Despite a decrease in power prices, the company achieved record-breaking power generation. Commencement of construction on the Svean hydropower plant signaled the start of Statkraft’s initiative to enhance and expand its Norwegian hydro and wind power sector. This was accompanied by subsequent investment commitments and requests for licenses.
news.headline: Statkraft reports record-high power generation and strong financial results in Q1 2025, begins development on new hydropower plant in Norway.

news.summary: On May 8, 2025, Vaalco Energy, Inc. (NYSE: EGY; LSE: EGY) declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.0625 per share of common stock for Q2 of 2025 ($0.25 annualized). The dividend will be paid on June 27, 2025, to stockholders of record on May 23, 2025. The Board of Directors will need to approve future dividend declarations and set record and payment dates.
news.headline: Vaalco Energy, Inc. declared a $0.0625 per share quarterly dividend ($0.25 annualized) payable on June 27, 2025.

news.summary: During the first quarter of 2025, Enefit Green group experienced a 3% decrease in operating income, alongside a significant 35% increase in operating expenses (excluding depreciation and amortization), in comparison to the same period in 2024. Consequently, the Earnings Before Interest, Taxes, Depreciation, and Amortization (EBITDA) saw a notable decline by 27%, reaching €31.0 million. The net profit for this period also took a hit, dropping by €11.8 million to €21.7 million. This translates to earnings per share of €0.082.
news.headline: Enefit Green Q1 2025: Operating income down 3%, EBITDA fell 27% to €31.0m, net profit down €11.8m. EPS €0.082.

news.summary: On May 8, 2025, The Hague-based company Aegon unveiled the agenda for its upcoming Annual General Meeting of Shareholders slated for June 12, 2025. The official meeting materials, encompassing the agenda, can now be accessed on the company’s website. During the AGM, the Board of Directors will showcase the 2024 Annual Accounts and propose a final dividend of EUR 0.19 per common share, making the total dividend for 2024 EUR 0.35 per common share. Additionally, proposals concerning the composition of Aegon Ltd.’s Board of Directors, previously disclosed on March 31, 2025, will be addressed. The AGM is set to occur in Bermuda, employing a hybrid format that allows shareholders to participate either in person or virtually. Attendees will have the opportunity to pose questions through live chat or video connection. Those not present physically or virtually can cast their votes in advance. Aegon will implement its policy on hybrid shareholder meetings. Detailed instructions on attendance, participation, and voting procedures can be found on the company’s website.
news.headline: Aegon's 2025 AGM agenda includes proposing a final dividend of EUR 0.19 per share and total dividend of EUR 0.35 per share for 2024. Meeting in Bermuda in hybrid style allowing in-person and virtual attendance with voting options.

news.summary: In Copenhagen, Denmark, on May 8, 2025, Novonesis, the top biosolutions provider worldwide, kicked off its financial year impressively, boasting a remarkable 11% uptick in organic sales. Additionally, they achieved an adjusted EBITDA margin of 38.3%. Looking ahead, the company remains confident in maintaining its full-year outlook.
news.headline: Novonesis reports 11% organic growth in Q1 with 38.3% EBITDA margin, maintains full-year outlook.

news.summary: A prominent neobank in the Philippines, Tonik, is leveraging the latest Agile methods and AI technologies in its software to propel growth. The bank is rapidly scaling up its operations, aiming to provide a superior customer experience and lead the way in digital banking in the country. The advancements are anticipated to significantly enhance the efficiency and effectiveness of the bank's services. This initiative is part of Tonik's strategic plan to stay at the forefront of innovation in the financial sector.
news.headline: Tonik neobank in Philippines achieves record growth, scaling at speed with Finastra's Essence software, enhancing customer experience.

news.summary: In Nairobi, Kenya, on May 8, 2025, Margaret Mliwa was named the new regional director of the Ford Foundation's East Africa office. The foundation, operating in Nairobi, is a nonpolitical entity focused on progress in the region.
news.headline: Ford Foundation appoints Margaret Mliwa as Regional Director for East Africa office in Nairobi.

news.summary: The G7 leaders might discuss North Korea's cyberattacks and crypto thefts at an upcoming summit in Canada, along with conflicts in Ukraine and Gaza. North Korea's increasing cyber threats have raised concerns, with hackers like the Lazarus Group stealing billions worth of crypto, including a $1.4 billion hack on Bybit in February. In 2024, North Korean-linked hackers stole over $1.3 billion through 47 crypto heists, per Chainalysis. The US, Japan, and South Korea have warned of North Korea's tech workers infiltrating crypto companies. Illicit proceeds from these hacks have helped North Korea fund its weapons programs. Lazarus-affiliated groups recently set up shell companies to scam crypto developers, while Kraken thwarted a North Korean hacker's attempt to infiltrate. This highlights the need for a coordinated international response to address North Korea's growing cyber threats.
news.headline: G7 summit may address North Korea's crypto hacks. Billions stolen in 2024, including $1.4B hack on Bybit. International response needed.

news.summary: Anheuser-Busch InBev reported strong financial results with a focus on their beer category, stating that consumer passion for beer continues to drive growth. In the recent quarter, the company's EBITDA increased within their projected range, and they saw a 7.1% growth in Underlying EPS. The company credited the profitable growth to the momentum of their megabrands and the optimization of their business operations. Anheuser-Busch InBev (Brussel:ABI) (BMV:ANB) (JSE:ANH) (NYSE:BUD) emphasized the importance of their strategy execution by their teams and partners in achieving these positive results.
news.headline: AB InBev reports Q1 2025 results with a 7.1% increase in Underlying EPS. EBITDA grew at the top-end of expectations.

news.summary: Bitcoin's realized capitalization hit a record high of $890 billion, reflecting strong investor conviction with long-term and short-term holders increasing their allocations. The surge in Bitcoin price saw a short squeeze above $97,000 on May 6 after US Treasury Secretary announced trade talks with China. The realized cap has seen three consecutive weeks of record-breaking growth, indicating a potential price breakout. Large BTC holders have been accumulating significantly since March 2025, with wallets adding 81,338 BTC over six weeks, signaling confidence in BTC's uptrend. The number of Bitcoin whale addresses holding over 1,000 BTC increased from 1,945 to 2,006 in the past month, marking the highest surge since Q1 2024. Bitcoin's support zone at $93,000-$95,000 showed resilience, while a breakout above $97,000-$99,000 could shift market dynamics and pave the way for a move toward $110,000. Analysts suggest the potential for BTC to reach $100,000 within the next two weeks, with some predicting a new all-time high of $111,111 by the end of Q2.
news.headline: Bitcoin's realized capitalization reaches $890B, with focus on reclaiming $100K level. Strong holder conviction shown. Whales accumulating BTC. Upside potential if resistance at $97K-$99K is broken, with a move towards $110K possible. Market optimism for BTC. Dip below $94K seen as a market bottom by some analysts. Predictions suggest new ATH of $111,111 in Q2.

news.summary: In Shanghai on May 7, 2025, a major development was announced by China's top fintech firm Qifu Technology (trading on NASDAQ as QFIN and HKEX as 3660) along with Beijing Jiaotong University. Together, they have reached a notable breakthrough with their research paper titled "Leveraging MLLM Embeddings and Attribute Smoothing for Compositional Zero-Shot."
news.headline: Qifu Technology's paper accepted by IJCAI 2025, using MLLM to innovate in fintech; collaboration with Beijing Jiaotong Univ.

news.summary: In the first quarter of 2025, there was a positive trend in gross profit across all regions, with Stockholm being a notable contributor. Net sales for January to March 2025 showed a 4 percent increase, reaching SEK 7,049 million compared to SEK 6,792 million in the same period last year. This growth equates to a 3 percent organic increase. Gross profit also saw a 4 percent rise, reaching SEK 2,408 million compared to SEK 2,312 million previously, which represents a 2 percent organic growth. These numbers indicate a robust performance and suggest a healthy business outlook.
news.headline: Sinch AB reports 4% increase in net sales to SEK 7,049m and 4% increase in gross profit to SEK 2,408m in Q1 2025.

news.summary: In Stockholm on May 8, 2025, Truecaller released its interim report for January-March 2025 showcasing a 16% rise in net sales to SEK 496.9 million from SEK 427.2 million. The company, a prominent global platform specializing in contact verification and spam communication prevention, highlighted growth across all revenue sources.
news.headline: Truecaller reports 16% increase in net sales to SEK 496.9 million (427.2) for Q1 2025, strong growth in revenues.

news.summary: Novo Nordisk A/S is seeking FDA approval for its oral version of the obesity drug Wegovy, with CEO Lars Fruergaard highlighting during a recent earnings call that the pill shows similar safety and efficacy to the injectable form. If approved, this oral weight loss drug will be the first of its kind in the U.S. market, being a GLP-1 treatment for obesity. The company aims to capitalize on being the first to market with a known molecule and proven efficacy and safety, carving out a significant position in the obesity treatment segment. The full story can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Novo Nordisk's 'Wegovy in a pill' shows '16.6%' average weight loss with over '20%' for one-third of adults struggling with obesity. CEO highlights potential FDA approval for first oral GLP-1 treatment in the US.

news.summary: Bitcoin is expected to break past $100,000 and potentially hit a new all-time high as US President Donald Trump is set to announce a trade deal with the UK. Trump's post on May 7 hinted at a major trade deal with the UK, causing Bitcoin to rise from $97,759 to $99,140. This rally is attributed to speculation surrounding the trade deal, with experts like Neil Jacobs and Anthony Pompliano believing it could lead to new record highs. Bitcoin last reached $100,000 on Feb. 1 and has seen significant growth, with $96 million in short positions liquidated due to a 3% increase over the past 24 hours. The Crypto & Fear Index shows a "Greed" score of 65, indicating bullish sentiment in the market. Readers are advised to conduct their own research before making any investment decisions.
news.headline: Bitcoin is closing in on $100K amid anticipation of a new all-time high after Trump hints at a trade deal with the UK. BTC currently priced at $99,140 per CoinMarketCap, expect new highs soon. Market sentiment is bullish, $96 million in short positions liquidated.

news.summary: According to analysis from Decrypt, traders are viewing a potential safety net for cryptocurrencies akin to a "Trump put." However, analysts caution that the current rally may face challenges if there is a lack of concrete policy implementation.
news.headline: Bitcoin surges to new highs, approaching $100K milestone. Traders cautious due to lack of policy implementation.

news.summary: Aker Horizons ASA, a Norwegian company specializing in green energy and industry development, revealed its first quarter 2025 outcomes today. news.headline: Aker Horizons ASA announced Q1 2025 results with net capital at NOK 4.3 billion, down NOK 1.7 billion.

news.summary: The latest report reveals that there has been a significant increase in COVID-19 cases in the past week, with a total of 10,000 new cases reported. The surge in infections has led to a spike in hospitalizations, with 500 new admissions in the last five days. Health officials are concerned about the rapid spread of the virus and are urging the public to follow safety guidelines to curb the rising numbers. This surge comes at a critical time as hospitals are already operating at 90% capacity, putting a strain on the healthcare system.
news.headline: Deutsche Bank strengthens defense finance with new team for deals.

news.summary: In a recent report, it was revealed that the unemployment rate in the country has decreased to 4.8% in the last month, marking a significant improvement from the previous rate of 5.2%. The number of new jobs created in the same period totaled 250,000, contributing to the overall economic growth. Additionally, the average hourly wages have also seen a slight increase of 2.5%, indicating a positive trend in the labor market. These figures highlight a promising outlook for the economy as it continues to recover from the challenges posed by the pandemic.
news.headline: Indian insurers are reportedly seeking relaxed rules for bond forwards to address market volatility.

news.summary: In recent developments, it has been reported that the stock market experienced a significant rise of 500 points today, reaching a record high of 30,000 points. This surge has been attributed to positive economic indicators and upbeat investor sentiment. Additionally, unemployment rates have decreased by 2% in the past month, with only 5% of the population currently jobless. Experts are optimistic about the future outlook of the economy and predict further growth in the coming months.

news.summary: A bill to create a "Texas Strategic Bitcoin Reserve" has been passed by a Texas House Committee with a 9-4 vote along party lines. The bill, Senate Bill 21, previously passed the Texas Senate with a 25-5 vote and now awaits a full floor vote before going to the governor's desk. The reserve would be controlled by the state's comptroller and would allow investments in digital assets with a market cap of at least $500 billion, currently only including Bitcoin. If successful, Texas Governor Greg Abbott, who has expressed interest in making Texas the "crypto capital" of the US, would sign the bill into law. The news comes as Arizona Governor signed a similar bill into law to establish a Bitcoin and Digital Asset Reserve Fund, using unclaimed crypto without taxpayer or state funds. Other states like New Hampshire have also passed laws allowing investments in cryptocurrencies with a market cap of over $500 billion, while Florida removed two Bitcoin reserve bills recently. The fate of the Texas bill should be determined before June 2.
news.headline: Texas House committee approves Republican-backed Bitcoin reserve bill, advances to full floor vote: 9-4 committee vote, 25-5 Senate vote. Governor approval pending. Other states also introducing similar bills.
